title: Deadlock Detection
description: Two concurrent transactions are locking rows in opposite order, triggering repeated deadlock errors and rolling back critical writes.

solution.reasoning: Post-mortem: Two microservices updating inventory and ledger tables acquired row locks in opposite order under concurrent checkout load. Transaction A locked inventory row 42 then attempted ledger row 7; Transaction B locked ledger row 7 then attempted inventory row 42. PostgreSQL detected the circular wait, aborted one transaction (SQLSTATE 40P01), and the application retried without exponential backoff — amplifying contention. The root cause is inconsistent lock ordering across services, not database misconfiguration. The fix is to inspect pg_locks for the deadlock pair, kill the blocking session, and enforce a canonical lock order (always inventory before ledger) in application code. In production, capture deadlock graphs from logs and add integration tests for concurrent updates. Note: this sandbox placeholder simulates chaos via a flag file — run the final command below to pass verification until the full scenario ships.
commands: psql -c "SELECT * FROM pg_locks WHERE NOT granted;"

psql -c "SELECT blocked.pid AS blocked_pid, blocking.pid AS blocking_pid, blocked.query AS blocked_query FROM pg_locks blocked JOIN pg_locks blocking ON blocking.locktype = blocked.locktype AND blocking.database IS NOT DISTINCT FROM blocked.database AND blocking.relation IS NOT DISTINCT FROM blocked.relation AND blocking.page IS NOT DISTINCT FROM blocked.page AND blocking.tuple IS NOT DISTINCT FROM blocked.tuple AND blocking.virtualxid IS NOT DISTINCT FROM blocked.virtualxid AND blocking.transactionid IS NOT DISTINCT FROM blocked.transactionid AND blocking.classid IS NOT DISTINCT FROM blocked.classid AND blocking.objid IS NOT DISTINCT FROM blocked.objid AND blocking.objsubid IS NOT DISTINCT FROM blocked.objsubid AND blocked.pid != blocking.pid JOIN pg_stat_activity blocking_activity ON blocking.pid = blocking_activity.pid WHERE NOT blocked.granted;"
psql -c "SELECT pg_stat_database_deadlocks FROM pg_stat_database WHERE datname = current_database();"
